name: Hub-Spoke Network Topology
strategicIntent: Provide a consistent, secure, and scalable network topology for all Azure workloads across McCain's global infrastructure.
problemStatement: Without a centralised network design, teams deploy isolated VNets with inconsistent security controls, creating governance gaps and increased attack surface.

useCases.use: Multi-spoke Azure landing zones needing centralised firewall inspection
useCases.dontUse: Single-VNet PoC environments with no inter-VNet routing needs

useCases.use: Production workloads requiring east-west traffic inspection
useCases.dontUse: Isolated dev sandboxes with no corporate data access

components: Azure Virtual WAN Hub — central routing and firewall integration
Azure Firewall Premium — TLS inspection and IDPS
Azure Private DNS Zones — internal name resolution
interactionSteps: Spoke VNet peers to regional hub via Virtual WAN
Azure Firewall inspects east-west and north-south traffic
DNS resolution routes through Private DNS Zones linked to hub
Diagnostic logs stream to Log Analytics workspace
securityConsiderations: All inter-spoke traffic must transit Azure Firewall for inspection
Network Security Groups must be applied to every subnet
TLS inspection enabled for outbound traffic via Firewall Premium
Private endpoints used for all PaaS service access
Network flow logs enabled for forensic analysis

guardrails.rule: All spoke VNets must peer to the regional hub
Azure Firewall must inspect all east-west traffic
NSG flow logs must be enabled on all subnets

exceptionProcess: Submit Architecture Review Board exception request with risk assessment, compensating controls, and target remediation date. CISO approval required for any network topology variance.

revisionHistory.author: Joe Bloggs
revisionHistory.summary: Added TLS inspection and Private DNS
